body.body:
Sub-ticket of T-substrate-experiment-cascade. From Akien's loop description: 'as soon as i spot the next lever, the experiment ends and the next one starts being designed.'

## The behavior

While a cascade level is running its probe, the probe may touch nodes that SURFACE new options Igor didn't have when the current experiment started. Example: level 1 widen-on-miss fires a word-graph neighbor expansion; one of the neighbors is an unexpected high-signal match that wasn't in the original hypothesis space. That match is a LEVER — a previously-invisible option Igor can now reach for. The correct move is to abort the current experiment and restart the cascade with a new hypothesis built around the lever.

Not completing the current probe is correct. The lever is higher-value than finishing the stale hypothesis.

## Scope

1. Probe functions optionally return `CascadeResult(status='leveraged', new_lever=Lever(...))`
2. Walker checks for leveraged status after each probe call
3. On lever detected: call `experiment.abort(reason='lever_detected:...')`, log the lever, restart the cascade with the lever as the new seed hypothesis
4. Lever detection rule (MVP): any result that wasn't predicted by the current hypothesis AND has relevance_score above a threshold
5. Per-turn budget: max N lever-interrupts per situation to prevent infinite flipping; after N, commit to the current best

## Biology

Basal ganglia + dopaminergic novelty bonus. A high-salience unexpected stimulus interrupts ongoing motor programs mid-execution. The interrupt is involuntary in biology; here it's a walker-level check after each probe step.

## CP grounding

- CP2 — aborting on a lever isn't failure; it's learning-ahead-of-completion
- CP3 — the abort reason is recorded, so the 'why' is auditable
- CP1 — if no lever is confident enough, the walker continues (humility default)

## Tests

- Probe returning a lever triggers abort
- Restarted cascade uses lever as new seed hypothesis
- Per-turn budget prevents flip-flopping
- Probes without levers run to normal exhaustion
- Abort reason recorded in Experiment.update.reason

## Depends on

T-substrate-experiment-cascade
body.title: Lever-interrupt — abort current experiment when new options surface mid-probe
body.result: Lever dataclass + CascadeResult.lever field + walker LEVERAGED handler with context accumulation, lever_budget default 3, lever_chain visibility via context + TWM marker. 14 tests. 2462 total pass.
